prompt: Chinese ink wash painting, Song dynasty Shuimo style (水墨畫), horizontal handscroll format. A long mountain range receding from left to right across the composition. The leftmost peak is rendered in dense, full-strength black ink — bold brushwork, strong tonal presence, clearly defined ridgelines and textures. Each successive peak behind it is painted with increasingly diluted ink: the second peak is dark grey, the third medium grey, the fourth pale grey, the fifth nearly invisible — a faint wash barely distinguishable from the white silk ground. The rightmost mountains at the far end of the range have almost vanished entirely into the white space, receiving almost no ink. Atmospheric perspective through ink dilution only — no linear perspective, no color, no Western shading. Empty white silk space dominates the upper portion of the composition. Mist pools between the peaks, accentuating their progressive disappearance. Minimal, deliberate brushstrokes — economy of mark. Each mountain is smaller and lighter than the last. Ink tones: dense sumi black at left transitioning through grey to near-white at right. No outlines filled with wash — the ink wash IS the form. No color, no decorative borders, no seals visible, no calligraphy, no text, no labels, no diagrams, no Western perspective, no photographic shading, no modern elements.
tradition_name: Chinese ink wash painting (Shuimo / 水墨畫)
tradition_period: Song dynasty through Ming dynasty, 960–1644 CE
tradition_approach: Applied atmospheric tonal recession through progressive ink dilution — dense sumi black at near distance fading to near-invisible wash at far distance — using economy of brushstroke, active negative white space, and no color or linear perspective.
